promptDescription: - **timearcs-gl** — WebGL-accelerated TimeArcs for large datasets. Same spec format and data semantics as `timearcs` (year/source/target/category/value, entity collapsing, force-ordered rows, year axis, activity bars, pan/zoom). Use this instead of `timearcs` whenever the result set is expected to exceed ~50k arcs (the plain SVG renderer drops frames around 10k–50k arcs and stalls into the hundreds of thousands; this renderer scales to millions). Identical SQL rules apply: lazy `"sql":"SELECT..."`, columns `year` (integer), `source`, `target`, `category` (STRONGLY RECOMMENDED — drives arc color coding and the per-category toggle legend; omitting it renders all arcs grey with no legend), optional `value`. For interactive time bucketing, also alias a raw timestamp/datetime column as `time` (col AS time, not pre-bucketed) to enable a TIME UNIT selector in the control panel (year/month/day/hour/minute/second); `year` remains the fallback when no `time` is provided. Always alias the category column as `category`. Spec: { "type":"timearcs-gl", "title":"...", "sql":"SELECT EXTRACT(year FROM col)::int AS year, src AS source, dst AS target, type AS category, COUNT(*) AS value FROM tbl GROUP BY 1,2,3,4", "timeRange":[startInt, endInt] }. Interaction notes vs `timearcs`: no per-arc hover tooltip; arc category colors and the toggle legend (click a category swatch to show/hide its arcs) work fully; row-label click for group expand/collapse also works. If the user needs per-arc tooltip on a small dataset, prefer plain `timearcs`.
